name: bc
title: bc
summary: GNU bc arbitrary precision calculator language
description:
GNU bc arbitrary precision calculator language
GNU bc is an interactive algebraic language with arbitrary precision which
follows the POSIX 1003.2 draft standard, with several extensions including
multi-character variable names, an `else' statement and full Boolean
expressions.  GNU bc does not require the separate GNU dc program.

use_cases: When you need Kali Linux, use bc because GNU bc arbitrary precision calculator language.
When the engagement is in the install phase, use bc as a cataloged Kali Linux tool with operator ACCEPT/CANCEL gating.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bc [ -hlwsqv ] [long-options] [  file ... ]` as documented upstream for bc.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bc always compute a value and return it to the caller.  Function definitions are "dynamic"  in` as documented upstream for bc.
When you need a concrete invocation, run `bc has one more special variable.  This special variable, history is the number  of  lines  of` as documented upstream for bc.
When chaining Kali Linux tools, pair bc with sibling catalog entries sharing its attack_surface/phase_hint.
When validating lab setup, run `bc --help` (or the README entrypoint) and confirm required hardware/deps from the catalog.
